subject: grandmas
one_liner: Cap table management for the family unit. Issue, vest, and dilute your grandchildren's stake in you.
brand_voice_notes: Straight-faced equity administration applied to a woman's affection for her descendants. Never sentimental, never winking. The product treats love as an unaudited liability and says so in the calm register of a compliance vendor. Grandchildren are holders. Christmas is a reporting period. A new baby is a financing event. The joke lands because nobody on the page finds any of it strange.

hero.h1: Your grandchildren are already shareholders. Nobody has been tracking it.
hero.sub: Nan is the system of record for familial equity. Issue shares, set vesting cliffs, and run a defensible valuation on the house before anyone brings up the ring at dinner.

features.title: 409A on the estate
features.blurb: An independent, audit-ready valuation of everything inside the house, refreshed quarterly. The good china is classified as a Level 3 asset because no observable market exists for it. The cabin is marked to the last offer your son-in-law made out loud.

features.title: Vesting cliffs for visits
features.blurb: Grandchildren vest over four years with a one-year cliff. A missed Christmas is recorded as a break in service. Reinstatement requires board approval, and the board is you, sitting in the chair by the window.

features.title: Dilution modeling
features.blurb: A new baby is a financing event. Nan models the round, shows every existing holder their revised percentage, and drafts the phone call so you do not have to find the words for it twice.

pricing_tiers.name: Sole Proprietor
pricing_tiers.tagline: For grandmothers with one grandchild and no disputes.
perks: Ledger goes read-only after a second holder is added

pricing_tiers.name: Matriarch
pricing_tiers.tagline: The standard configuration for a family that still eats together.
perks: One contested Thanksgiving per year, mediated

pricing_tiers.name: Founding Family Office
pricing_tiers.tagline: For estates where the word lawyer has already been said out loud.
perks: Board observer seat for the executor
Quiet period enforced through the holidays

testimonials.quote: I issued to all eleven of them on a Tuesday. Two of them called me back.
testimonials.name: Marguerite Odom
testimonials.title: Sole Director, Odom Family Holdings

testimonials.quote: I found out my sister's kids were sitting on preferred. I have been common this entire time.
testimonials.name: Denise Aughtry
testimonials.title: Grandchild, Class B, Unvested

testimonials.quote: We ran the numbers and the cabin is worth less than the resentment. Nan tracked both line items.
testimonials.name: Raymond Pesh
testimonials.title: Executor and Middle Son

demo_screen_concept: The Nan cap table: a ledger of eleven grandchildren as equity holders. Each row shows the holder's name, share class chip (Common, Preferred, or Founder's Grandchild), shares outstanding, fully-diluted ownership percentage, a vesting progress bar with a visible one-year cliff marker, and days since last contact. The table filters by share class and sorts by ownership. Two live controls sit above it: an 'Issue shares' modal that adds a holder and recomputes every percentage, and a 'Model a new grandchild' dilution simulator with a slider that live-recalculates the entire cap table as the new holder's stake grows, flagging any holder who drops below their anti-dilution floor.

images.rationale: Claymorphism is an illustrative style, so a clay-render hero earns its slot: a molded grandmother seated with a ledger, which is the whole premise in one frame. Demo subject assets skipped because the demo screen is a data ledger (table rows, vesting bars, a dilution slider), not photo-shaped. Remaining budget went to three clay-render testimonial headshots, one per named holder, rendered in the same molded-polymer medium so they read as part of the page rather than stock photography. All four passed vision judging on the first attempt.

items.prompt: Soft matte clay 3D render, claymorphic style: a single elderly woman seated in a high-backed armchair in a quiet parlour, holding a closed ledger book on her lap, calm neutral expression, facing slightly off-camera. Everything modeled as smooth molded polymer clay with rounded chunky forms and no sharp edges. Pale lavender-clay background #F1ECF7, heirloom plum #6B4E8C for the armchair and her cardigan, one small accent of estate coral #C9503A on the ledger cover. Soft studio lighting from the upper left, gentle paired shadows, low contrast, matte surfaces with no gloss or specular highlights. Single focal subject, generous empty space around her. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Soft matte clay 3D render portrait, claymorphic style: head-and-shoulders corporate headshot of an elderly woman with short silver hair and reading glasses, composed neutral expression, facing camera directly. Modeled as smooth molded polymer clay, rounded chunky forms, no sharp edges. Flat pale lavender-clay background #F1ECF7, her blazer in heirloom plum #6B4E8C. Soft even studio lighting from the upper left, gentle paired shadows, matte surfaces with no gloss. Single focal subject, centered, tight crop. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Soft matte clay 3D render portrait, claymorphic style: head-and-shoulders corporate headshot of a woman in her early forties with dark shoulder-length hair, flat unimpressed expression, facing camera directly. Modeled as smooth molded polymer clay, rounded chunky forms, no sharp edges. Flat pale lavender-clay background #F1ECF7, her top in muted mauve #8A7C9C. Soft even studio lighting from the upper left, gentle paired shadows, matte surfaces with no gloss. Single focal subject, centered, tight crop. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Soft matte clay 3D render portrait, claymorphic style: head-and-shoulders corporate headshot of a balding man in his mid-fifties with a close-trimmed grey beard, tired resigned expression, facing camera directly. Modeled as smooth molded polymer clay, rounded chunky forms, no sharp edges. Flat pale lavender-clay background #F1ECF7, his shirt in deep plum ink #3B2E4A. Soft even studio lighting from the upper left, gentle paired shadows, matte surfaces with no gloss. Single focal subject, centered, tight crop. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Claymorphic family-office console, heirloom-parlour daylight mode. The page is staged as the equity-administration platform of a heavily funded cap-table company that has decided a grandmother's affection is an untracked security and prices its administration by the seat. A pale lavender-clay ground carries soft drifting blob forms at very low opacity, so the page reads as molded, pressed material rather than paper or glass. Every panel is a true clay slab: chunky 28px radii, a flat pastel fill lifted off the ground by a paired shadow system, one soft dark shadow below-right at 18px blur and one soft white highlight above-left at 12px blur, plus a faint inset rim so the surface looks inflated rather than printed. No blur veils, no transparency, no borders heavier than 2px, no bevels, no screws, no paper grain, no hairline rules. One heirloom plum carries the wordmark, headlines, structural labels, and all institutional text, so the administrative voice reads as soft-set clay. One rationed estate coral, the color of a price sticker at a family sale, is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA per section, the live ledger-status dot, the break-in-service badge, and any holder who has fallen below their anti-dilution floor, and appears almost nowhere else as flat fill. Every figure that pretends to be equity telemetry (share counts, ownership percentages, vesting progress, days since last contact, valuations, tier prices) is set in DM Mono with tabular figures inside pressed inset wells, so it reads like a ledger readout, not marketing. Fredoka sets the wordmark and headlines in rounded display with the unbothered confidence of a fintech that has never been asked whether this is appropriate; Nunito carries plain, faintly clerical body copy. Motion is soft and physical: blobs drift on a 26s loop, the ledger dot pulses on a 3s cycle, slabs rise 8px and fade in on scroll, buttons squish to scale 0.96 and compress their shadow on press, vesting bars fill once on scroll, the dilution slider recomputes with a spring settle, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No dark mode, no glass, no blur, no neon, no bento lattice, no brutalist rules, no serifs, no marquee, no emoji. Reads like a company that files a grandmother's love under 'unissued options' and lists a grandson who stopped calling as a break in service.
design_direction.archetype: Fintech/SaaS product landing page: hero with product plate, three-feature slab row, testimonial slabs, three-tier pricing ladder, closing CTA.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Soft clay physics. Blob drift on a 26s loop, 3s ledger-dot pulse, 8px rise-and-fade slabs on scroll, scale 0.96 squish with shadow compression on press, one-time vesting-bar fill, spring settle on the dilution slider. All gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left column carries the wordmark, a mono eyebrow label (LEDGER · ACTIVE) with the coral status dot, the h1 in Fredoka at clamp scale, the sub in Nunito, and the single coral CTA. Right column carries a clay hero plate: an inset ledger well showing three abbreviated holder rows with share-class chips and a partially filled vesting bar, floating on a raised clay slab. A soft drifting blob sits behind the plate at low opacity.
